events.title: Seoul raises terror alert as it prepares to host BTS comeback concert
events.description: Thousands of police prepare to deploy to South Korea’s capital ahead of K-pop’s most anticipated comeback Seoul has stepped up security ahead of BTS’s huge comeback concert on Saturday, which more than a quarter of a million fans are expected to attend, with authorities raising the terror alert in the area and preparing to deploy thousands of police to the capital. South Korean president Lee Jae Myung warned at a cabinet meeting this week that “the issue is safety” and urged heightened vigilance by the interior ministry and emergency services to prepare for every possibility. He described the concert as an important occasion to reaffirm the country’s global cultural standing. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Seoul raises terror alert as it prepares to host BTS comeback concert

events.title: Energy prices soar, Iran and US trade threats after Qatar gas hit
events.description: Oil and gas prices soared Thursday after Iran hit the world's largest liquefied natural gas (LNG) facility in Qatar and threatened to destroy the region's energy infrastructure. Donald Trump, whose country started the war alongside Israel with their attack on Iran on February 28, warned of a furious US response if such attacks continued. International benchmark Brent surged 10 percent before falling back while European gas rose 35 percent after Iran attacked Qatar's huge Ras Laffan LNG facility in retaliation for an Israeli strike on its South Pars gas field. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Angela Diffley tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Energy prices soar, Iran and US trade threats after Qatar gas hit

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise
events.description: Ongoing strikes across the Middle East stemming from the US-Israeli bombing of Iran and subsequent counterstrikes in the Gulf by Tehran continue to drive humanitarian needs, civilian casualties and mass displacement. Concerns are also growing over maritime security, the economic crisis centred around the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, attacks on healthcare and the protection of critical infrastructure, including nuclear facilities. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 19 March: Civilians bear the brunt as strikes and displacement rise

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 18 March: Lebanon displacement tops one million as nuclear safety concerns raised
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with ongoing strikes and rising humanitarian needs. The UN nuclear watchdog reported Wednesday that a projectile struck the site of Iran’s Bushehr nuclear power plant, although no casualties have been reported. In Lebanon, the humanitarian situation is worsening, with hundreds reported killed and more than one million people displaced. UN agencies are scaling up emergency assistance despite mounting challenges. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 18 March: Lebanon displacement tops one million as nuclear safety concerns raised

events.title: At least 23 people killed in suspected suicide attacks in north-eastern Nigeria
events.description: More than 100 others injured in bombings targeting post office, market areas and hospital in Maiduguri At least 23 people have been killed and more than 100 others injured in multiple suspected suicide bombings in the north-eastern Nigerian city of Maiduguri, shattering its reputation as a relative oasis of calm in recent years as a long-running insurgency was pushed to the rural hinterlands. Authorities said the explosions went off at the post office and market areas, as well as the entrance to the University of Maiduguri teaching hospital, on Monday evening during iftar, the breaking of fast in the month of Ramadan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 At least 23 people killed in suspected suicide attacks in north-eastern Nigeria

events.title: Trump’s threats to ‘take’ Cuba signal rising US pressure as island grapples with power crisis
events.description: National power outage is making life extremely difficult and may force Havana into biggest economic changes in 67 years Just a few hours after a nationwide electricity blackout struck Cuba, Donald Trump hinted at an even darker future for the island’s rulers. The country’s entire electricity system had collapsed on Monday afternoon, leaving about 10 million people without power. Emergency teams were still struggling to restore it when the US leader made his latest threat. Continue reading...

events.title: Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell
events.description: Nigeria had largest increase in terrorism-related deaths, ranking fourth in global index behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger Jihadist violence rose sharply in Nigeria and Democratic Republic of Congo last year, even as global deaths from terrorism dropped to their lowest level in a decade, according to a new report. Nigeria recorded the largest increase in terrorism deaths globally in 2025, with fatalities rising by 46% from 513 in 2024 to 750, placing it fourth in the Global Terrorism Index, behind Pakistan, Burkina Faso and Niger. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Jihadist violence in Nigeria and DRC rose sharply last year even as global deaths from terror fell

events.title: Steven Everts: For Europeans, war on Iran 'a diversion' and 'giant gift to Putin'
events.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Steven Everts, Director of European Union Institute for Security Studies (EUISS). According to Mr. Everts, the current conflict is an unnecessary war defined by cycles of retaliation that risk expanding beyond their original scope, with serious implications for regional stability and global energy markets. Europe is already feeling the economic fallout, but the deeper concern lies in the possibility of further destabilisation. He sees this war as strategically counterproductive, not only for Europe but for broader international stability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Steven Everts: For Europeans, war on Iran 'a diversion' and 'giant gift to Putin'

events.title: War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief
events.description: The disruption of global energy supplies is being felt worldwide, the UN’s top climate change official warned on Monday, as conflict in the Middle East drives oil and gas prices sharply higher – echoing the market turmoil triggered by the war in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War-driven energy price spikes highlight value of renewables: UN climate chief

events.title: Fuel rations and no air con: south-east Asian nations race to conserve energy
events.description: Governments in countries heavily reliant on Middle Eastern oil introduce measures to shield public from soaring costs Middle East crisis – live updates In Thailand, news anchors ditched their jackets on air as the government called on the public to reduce their use of air conditioning to save energy. In the Philippines, many government workers are now operating on a four-day week. In Vietnam, officials have urged employers to allow staff to work from home. Across south-east Asia, governments are scrambling to find ways to conserve energy and shield the public from soaring costs as war in the Middle East causes what the International Energy Agency has described as the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fuel rations and no air con: south-east Asian nations race to conserve energy

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East continues, with strikes and counterstrikes reported across the region as the humanitarian toll rises. UN agencies warn that health systems are under growing strain, children are increasingly affected by violence and displacement, and hundreds of thousands have been forced from their homes - especially in Lebanon. Despite mounting logistical and security challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 17 March: Crisis impact ripples across region

events.title: World News in Brief: Deadly drone strikes in Sudan, families killed in West Bank, Ukraine casualties update
events.description: Relentless drone strikes across Sudan’s Kordofan and Darfur regions are causing civilian casualties and further restricting humanitarian access, the UN warned on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Deadly drone strikes in Sudan, families killed in West Bank, Ukraine casualties update

events.title: Malinauskas steps up One Nation attack ahead of SA election – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Tropical Cyclone Narelle tracker map: where and when will it hit the Queensland coast? Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Agriculture minister says government monitoring any price gouging of fertiliser Julie Collins , the federal minister for agriculture, said the government is monitoring any price gouging for fertiliser amid the turmoil in the Middle East. I think our government has been very clear that this should not be seen as a commercial opportunity for anybody. This is about what is in the national interest. This is a conflict that is impacting globally, and what we need Australians to do is to act in the national interest, and that includes everybody along that supply chain. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Malinauskas steps up One Nation attack ahead of SA election – as it happened

events.title: Settler violence surges in the West Bank
events.description: As the Iran-Israel-US-Lebanon war makes headlines around the world, what is happening in the occupied West Bank? Expropriation of Palestinian land, settler attacks and killings have long been the story of the West Bank, but human rights campaigners and experts say that amid the fog of regional war, attacks are on the rise and Israeli settlers are emboldened. We take a closer look in this special edition of Middle East Matters.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Settler violence surges in the West Bank

events.title: 'Disaster: Attacks on gas infrastructure, in Iran and Qatar, may set us back several years'
events.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Adi Imsirovic, Author, Director at Surrey Clean Energy and Lecturer on Energy Systems at Oxford University. According to Dr.Imsirovic, we are witnessing a structural rupture in the norms that have quietly governed conflict in energy-producing regions for decades. Critical energy infrastructure was largely kept off the battlefield, until now. This boundary has now been breached and the consequences will affect the global supply chains for years to come.

events.title: Deadly blasts at market and hospital raise fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency in Nigeria
events.description: Nigeria suicide bombings in Maiduguri killed at least 23 people and wounded over 100 others in deadly attacks targeting a hospital and market Monday night.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly blasts at market and hospital raise fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency in Nigeria

events.title: 'Not our war': Palestinians mourn first dead after Iran missile fire
events.description: The blast struck without warning in the Palestinian town of Beit Awa, sending a hail of missile fragments through a beauty salon and killing four women inside. In the Israeli-occupied West Bank, the first Palestinian deaths in the Middle East war have sharpened a sense of helplessness as they find themselves ensnared in the crossfire of yet another conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Not our war': Palestinians mourn first dead after Iran missile fire

events.title: Millions of children out of school: Yemen's education system on its knees
events.description: Since 2015 and the outbreak of war between the Houthis, who control the north of Yemen, and the internationally recognised government in the south, the country's education system has been steadily collapsing. According to UNICEF, nearly 4 million Yemeni children are currently out of school, amounting to 40 percent of the under-18 population.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Millions of children out of school: Yemen's education system on its knees

events.title: What we know about Joe Kent - the top US counterterrorism official who has resigned over Trump's Iran war
events.description: Donald Trump's most senior counterterrorism official has resigned, saying he "cannot in good conscience" support the Iran war and claiming the country posed "no imminent threat" to the United States.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What we know about Joe Kent - the top US counterterrorism official who has resigned over Trump's Iran war

events.title: Fact check: How doctored images from Iran misled media outlets
events.description: AI‑generated Iran war images fooled international media outlets, including DW, exposing how easily manipulated images can infiltrate global newsrooms. Here is what we learned and how you can spot the fake images.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fact check: How doctored images from Iran misled media outlets

events.title: Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field
events.description: #Israel hit Iranian's part of the South Pars #gas field, the world's largest offshore reservoir #Tehran shares with #Doha, on March 18. In retaliation for the strikes, #Iran ramped up its attacks on Gulf countries' energy infrastructures, sending #oil prices soaring. Israel also reported strikes on its refineries on March 19.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war escalates after attack on key Iranian gas field

events.title: Chad says 17 killed in Sudanese drone attack
events.description: Officials in Chad have said those killed were civilians. Locals say they were mourners at a funeral and children playing nearby. The attack is the latest deadly spillover from the devastating civil war raging in Sudan.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Chad says 17 killed in Sudanese drone attack

events.title: At least 17 dead in fighting on Sudan's border with Chad, aid group says
events.description: Attacks on a town along Sudan’s border with Chad have killed at least 17 people and injured 123, Doctors Without Borders, or MSF, said

events.title: Middle East war risks undermining Syria’s fragile recovery, Security Council hears
events.description: The international community must step up efforts to support Syria amid the current conflict raging in the Middle East, two senior UN officials told the Security Council on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war risks undermining Syria’s fragile recovery, Security Council hears

events.title: Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul
events.description: Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan’s overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul, according to Taliban authorities, and injured more than 250 others.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul

events.title: Hero police officers storm inferno to save 5 passengers from fireball crash
events.description: Michigan police officers heroically rescued five people from a fiery car crash in Canton Township, and four officers were injured during the rescue.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Hero police officers storm inferno to save 5 passengers from fireball crash

events.title: Former counter-terrorism head investigated by FBI over alleged leaks
events.description: The former counter-terrorism head resigned on Tuesday over the war with Iran, saying the country posed "no imminent threat" to the US.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former counter-terrorism head investigated by FBI over alleged leaks

events.title: Isolated and exposed: can New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery withstand the global oil shock?
events.description: New Zealand economic growth tipped to overtake Australia’s this year but Middle East conflict casts a shadow over outlook Just as New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery shows flickers of improvement – with economists predicting its annual growth could surpass that of its larger neighbour Australia – it is facing a new threat: the war in the Middle East. New Zealand is particularly exposed to the energy shocks produced by the conflict – and to economic crises generally – with the small, isolated nation highly dependent on global trade and tourism. It is susceptible to disruptions in supply chains and shipping. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Isolated and exposed: can New Zealand’s fragile economic recovery withstand the global oil shock?

events.title: Celebrating the diversity of 'la langue française'
events.description: You’ll hear its unmistakable sound in tiny islands of the South Pacific, bustling capitals in West Africa and chilly ports in the northeast of the Americas. The French language is spoken by more than 390 million people all over the world, and that linguistic community is celebrated by the International Organisation of Francophonie. French content is also increasingly popular on social media, with lifestyle, culture and education influencers sharing linguistic tips online.

events.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing hundreds in Kabul hospital strike
events.description: Reported airstrike on Afghanistan hospital allegedly kills 400+ civilians, sparking widespread criticism over muted international response to the attack.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of killing hundreds in Kabul hospital strike

events.title: As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war
events.description: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war

events.title: Middle East war’s ‘spiral of conflict’ drives mounting civilian toll
events.description: The widening war in the Middle East and its growing impact on civilians came under scrutiny at the UN in Geneva on Monday, as independent experts briefing the Human Rights Council warned of escalating violence following the onset of Israeli and US strikes on Iran and counterstrikes by Tehran and allied groups.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war’s ‘spiral of conflict’ drives mounting civilian toll

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 March: UN scales up aid as crisis deepens into third week
events.description: The crisis in the Middle East has entered its third week, with fighting continuing across the region and humanitarian needs rising. Oil prices continue to hover above $100 a barrel, while shipping disruptions and temporary flight suspensions are affecting travel and supply chains. Despite mounting challenges, UN agencies are scaling up emergency aid, warning that displacement, food insecurity and pressure on health systems are growing as the war's impact spreads. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 16 March: UN scales up aid as crisis deepens into third week

events.title: Canada in push for joint G7 and Middle East effort to de-escalate Iran war
events.description: Foreign minister Anita Anand says she has drafted principles to reduce risk of regional spillover and wider shocks Middle East crisis – live updates Canada is pushing for a collective G7 and Middle East approach to de-escalating the Iran war, including off ramps that could bring an end to the conflict, the Canadian foreign minister, Anita Anand, has said. In London to meet the UK foreign secretary, Yvette Cooper, after talks with her Turkish counterpart, Hakan Fidan, Anand told the Guardian she hoped a G7 meeting chaired by France, this year’s president of the group, might start to build a broader collective approach to the crisis. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada in push for joint G7 and Middle East effort to de-escalate Iran war

events.title: Israel suspected of using war weapon illegally in south Lebanon
events.description: More than a million people in Lebanon have been displaced and over 900 killed as Israel intensifies its offensive against the militant group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel suspected of using war weapon illegally in south Lebanon

events.title: World News in Brief: West Bank displacement, Cuba fuel crisis, sexual abuse safeguards, new ‘humancentric’ AI advocate
events.description: A new UN human rights report warns that expanding Israeli settlements are driving large-scale displacement of Palestinians across the occupied West Bank.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: West Bank displacement, Cuba fuel crisis, sexual abuse safeguards, new ‘humancentric’ AI advocate

events.title: Next US move on Iran: Seize Kharg Island, secure uranium or risk ground war escalation

events.title: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar
events.description: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar

events.title: Iran war exposes Gulf economies' food, water vulnerability
events.description: The spiralling conflict in Iran has highlighted the potential vulnerability of Gulf states when it comes to food and water security. Countries like Bahrain and Qatar depend heavily on desalinated water, and strikes on desalination plants have sparked concern about water supplies. Meanwhile, the usual flow of food imports through the Strait of Hormuz has been disrupted by the fighting.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war exposes Gulf economies' food, water vulnerability

events.title: Iran’s hidden mountain nuclear site raises urgent threat, must be ‘neutralized': reports
events.description: Andrea Stricker warns the U.S. must neutralize Iran's Pickaxe Mountain nuclear site and secure uranium stockpiles before ending combat operations.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s hidden mountain nuclear site raises urgent threat, must be ‘neutralized': reports

events.title: The Iran war is causing a global energy crisis - can China withstand it?
events.description: With oil supply disrupted, Beijing's oil reserves and renewable energy push are being put to the test.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The Iran war is causing a global energy crisis - can China withstand it?

events.title: Drone attack from Sudan kills 17 people in Chad as war spills over border
events.description: Local resident says casualties include mourners at funeral and children playing nearby.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Drone attack from Sudan kills 17 people in Chad as war spills over border

events.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities. And, Cesar Chavez accused of sexual abuse, rape
events.description: Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.

events.title: Iran war: Why is the South Pars gas field so important?
events.description: An attack on Iran's key South Pars gas field has rattled global energy markets, raised fears of further escalation and driven oil prices even higher.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Why is the South Pars gas field so important?

events.title: Iran war: Tehran vows 'zero restraint' if energy sites hit
events.description: Iran has threatened greater retaliation against strikes on its energy infrastructure. Meanwhile, President Trump has threatened to "blow up" Iran's South Pars gas if attacks against Qatar continue. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Tehran vows 'zero restraint' if energy sites hit

events.title: 'Free France': Macron reveals name of Europe's largest warship
events.description: President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday announced that France's next nuclear-powered aircraft carrier will be called "France Libre" (Free France), as the country looks to reinforce its status as a major maritime power. Once completed, the warship, which is set to replace the country's sole aircraft carrier – the Charles de Gaulle – and due to enter service in 2038, will be the largest warship ever built in Europe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Free France': Macron reveals name of Europe's largest warship

events.title: Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger
events.description: The Middle East war could cause the worst disruption to lifesaving humanitarian work since COVID, the UN World Food Programme (WFP) warned on Tuesday, as the UN chief again demanded an end to the widening conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger

events.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
events.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack

events.title: Death toll surpasses 1,000 in Lebanon as Israeli bombardment continues
events.description: At least 40 medical workers among those killed as rights groups urge Israel to end attacks on Lebanon health facilities.

events.title: Why are Iran’s South Pars gasfield, Qatar’s Ras Laffan, so significant?
events.description: Israel's strike on South Pars and Iran's attack on Ras Laffan escalate energy crisis, deepen regional tensions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why are Iran’s South Pars gasfield, Qatar’s Ras Laffan, so significant?

events.title: Iran: Saudi Arabia warns of 'military action' over repeated attacks
events.description: Saudi Arabia warned that it had 'reserved the right to take 'military actions' over #Iran's repeated attacks on the kingdom, as fresh trikes hit Riyadh during a summit of regional foreign ministers. The comment marks a turn in the #Gulf's reaction to Iran's strikes on its neighbours since the start of the #war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran: Saudi Arabia warns of 'military action' over repeated attacks

events.title: Greece launches an animal airlift to evacuate pets and their owners from the Mideast
events.description: Dozens of dogs and cats have arrived in Athens with their owners aboard a special evacuation flight for Greeks with pets who were trapped by the current war in the Middle East
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Greece launches an animal airlift to evacuate pets and their owners from the Mideast

events.title: ‘Hope’ for Haiti’s political future as some 300 groups reportedly register for upcoming elections
events.description: Haiti remains mired in a multidimensional crisis marked by weak institutions, political uncertainty, widespread gang violence and overwhelming humanitarian needs, but a recent new agreement by political groups offers “a moment of hope and progress for the Haitian people”, according to the UN’s most senior official in the Caribbean country.

events.title: Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed
events.description: Video footage captured fiery destruction and emergency crews responding after an Iranian missile strike near Tel Aviv, Israel, killed two people.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fiery aftermath of Iran missile strike near Tel Aviv caught on video after 2 killed

events.title: Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal
events.description: If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal

events.title: Six nations say ready to boost 'efforts to ensure safe passage' in Hormuz
events.description: Six Western allies, including Britain, France, Germany and Japan, said in a joint statement Thursday they were ready "to contribute to appropriate efforts to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz". The statement came as around 20,000 seafarers remained stuck on approximately 3,200 vessels west of the Strait of Hormuz, according to the International Maritime Organization, due to an effective Iranian blockade of the crucial maritime chokepoint. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

events.title: As Cuba struggles with power cuts, how is the island holding up?
events.description: The BBC's Will Grant reports from the island days after the entire national electric grid collapsed.

events.title: 'Idea that Trump can be led around by Israel is foolishness,' ex-envoy to Iran says
events.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Elliott Abrams, a former special envoy of US President Donald Trump to Iran, dismissed as "foolishness" the idea that Israel led Trump into war with Tehran. Abrams, who served during Trump's first term in office, said he backed the ongoing war, saying that "five presidents in a row" had vowed to prevent a nuclear Iran but Trump "is the first person who's really acting on that".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Idea that Trump can be led around by Israel is foolishness,' ex-envoy to Iran says

events.title: 12 Arab and Islamic countries unite to condemn 'heinous' Iranian attacks
events.description: A coalition of 12 Arab and Islamic countries denounces Iran’s attacks on civilian infrastructure, urges restraint and warns of risks to regional security.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 12 Arab and Islamic countries unite to condemn 'heinous' Iranian attacks

events.title: Belgium deploys military to guard Jewish sites after Iran-linked group claims Europe attacks
events.description: Belgium deploys military to protect Jewish communities after explosion outside Liège, Belgium, synagogue. Officials call blast a "despicable antisemitic" act.

events.title: REPLAY: Pentagon press conference on war in Middle East
events.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Thursday there is no "time frame" for ending the US-Israeli war against Iran, which was launched three weeks ago. "We wouldn't want to set a definitive time frame," Hegseth told a news conference, adding that "we're very much on track" and that President Donald Trump will be the one to decide when to stop. Hegseth also addressed a report that the Pentagon has requested more than $200 billion in additional funding from Congress to pay for the conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 REPLAY: Pentagon press conference on war in Middle East

events.title: Top Iranian official, commander killed in strike, Israel defense minister says
events.description: Iran's Supreme National Security Council secretary Ali Larijani and Basij Commander Gholamreza Soleimani have been "eliminated," the Israel Defense Forces said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Top Iranian official, commander killed in strike, Israel defense minister says

events.title: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17

events.title: Watch: Gabbard faces scrutiny over Iran nuclear intelligence claims
events.description: The US Director of National Intelligence, Tulsi Gabbard, was questioned over what Democrats perceive as a discrepancy between White House and intelligence community claims.

events.title: Could Iran war trigger the next global food shock?

events.title: Iran War: Gulf states debate US bases, military alliance
events.description: The view that the US didn't consult regional allies before going to war with Iran, then Iran's retaliation, are raising doubts in the Gulf states: Did hosting US bases make them safer? Or did it make them a target?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran War: Gulf states debate US bases, military alliance

events.title: Impact of US-Israel war with Iran on Turkish tourism
events.description: Turkey is one of the most cherished vacation spots in the world. In 2024, it was the fourth most popular destination. But there has been a flurry of cancellations in the wake of the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Impact of US-Israel war with Iran on Turkish tourism

events.title: Social media makes people unhappy — World Happiness Report
events.description: The 2026 World Happiness Report found that life satisfaction among those under 25 in some English-speaking countries has fallen sharply over the past decade. Finland was once again the happiest country.

events.title: World News in Brief: Yemen appeal, Middle East war roils Somalia, needs grow in Colombia
events.description: UN aid teams in Yemen on Wednesday warned that the situation in the war-torn nation remains dire in a call for $2 billion in aid to deliver lifesaving assistance for 12 million people.

events.title: “It’s time to end the war.” UN’s Guterres urges US, Israel to cease attacks

events.title: Nuclear's cleanup cost threatens the expansion dream
events.description: Countries are racing to build new reactors. But we've barely figured out how to clean up the old ones — and the bill is potentially staggering.

events.title: Turkey: Thousands of Imamoglu supporters rally in Istanbul
events.description: One year after his arrest on terrorism and corruption charges, thousands of supporters of jailed Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu gathered to protest. In a pre-written message, Imamoglu slammed a "corrupt mindset."
events.ticker_label: 💥 Turkey: Thousands of Imamoglu supporters rally in Istanbul

events.title: Former counterterrorism chief Joe Kent under FBI investigation and more top headlines

events.title: Israel says Haifa oil refinery hit in Iranian missile attack

events.title: Missile strikes close to reporter during Israeli attacks in Lebanon

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: 'My children became my purpose': Author Stuart J. Green on rebuilding after trauma
events.description: A man who lost his wife after she was shot dead in her car in the Philippines a decade ago with their children in the back seat has spoken to FRANCE 24 of the trauma of such an event, and how his family has got through what happened. Stuart J. Green lost not only his wife, but also his job and the country he'd been living in for 25 years, leaving him with three traumatised children to look after. He found little help in the world of literature, so he decided to write his own book to try to help others facing extreme trauma. Green is the author of "The Regenerate Leap" and spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: Apple issues spyware alert to iPhone users
events.description: Apple is urging users to update their iPhones after the discovery of new spyware that can take over phones running older versions of the iOS operating system.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Apple issues spyware alert to iPhone users

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle to make landfall in far north Qld on Friday as category 5 storm, bringing 315km/h wind gusts
events.description: Massive storm tracking a path to Queensland coast, which intensified offshore Thursday morning to category 5, fuelled by warm waters in Coral Sea Cyclone Narelle path tracker map Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle is expected to make landfall in far north Queensland on Friday morning as a monster category 5 storm, bringing destructive wind gusts of 250km/h, according to the Bureau of Meteorology. The severe cyclone rapidly intensified over the past 48 hours and in the early hours of Friday morning had built to a category 5 storm that was barrelling west, sitting about 150km east of the small town of Coen. Coen has a population of approximately 330. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Tropical Cyclone Narelle to make landfall in far north Qld on Friday as category 5 storm, bringing 315km/h wind gusts

events.title: What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war

events.ticker_label: 🪖 What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war

events.title: Paris mayoral race: Socialist frontrunner faces fierce attacks from rivals
events.description: The Paris mayoral race narrowed on Monday as a former minister hoping to wrest control of the French capital from the left sealed a deal to join forces with a fellow right-winger for the second-round run-off. Her main rival is Socialist former deputy mayor Emmanuel Gregoire, who came out well ahead of her in the first round of voting on Sunday. FRANCE 24's French Politics Editor Marc Perelman tells us more.

events.title: Senegal to appeal CAF Africa Cup of Nations decision after team stripped of title
events.description: Senegal's football federation said Thursday that it would appeal the Confederation of African Football (CAF) decision to strip the country's team of its Africa Cup of Nations and award the title to Morocco. Senegal beat Morocco in Rabat on January 18, a victory that was short-lived after CAF reversed Senegal's win two months later.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Senegal to appeal CAF Africa Cup of Nations decision after team stripped of title

events.title: Some of the world’s poorest countries to lose UK aid due to 56% budget cut
events.description: UK’s bilateral aid to Africa, which funds areas such as schools and clinics, to be cut by almost £900m by 2028-29 Some of the world’s poorest countries will lose out on UK aid that funds programmes such as schools and clinics, due to budget cuts set out by the foreign secretary. The UK’s bilateral aid to Africa will be reduced by almost £900m by 2028-29 – a 56% cut – part of more than £6bn in cuts which are funding an increase in defence spending. Continue reading...

events.title: Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader
events.description: Family of then PM, Patrice Lumumba, welcome decision to charge Étienne Davignon as ‘beginning of a reckoning’ A former Belgian diplomat, 93, should stand trial over alleged complicity in the 1961 murder of Patrice Lumumba, the first prime minister of what was then the newly independent Congolese state, a Brussels court has ruled. Étienne Davignon, the only person still alive among 10 Belgians the Lumumba family accuses of involvement in the killing, is charged with participation in war crimes. The illegal transfer of Lumumba and his associates from Léopoldville (now Kinshasa) to Katanga. The “humiliating and degrading treatment” of the men. Depriving them of a fair trial. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Belgian court sends ex-diplomat, 93, to trial over 1961 murder of Congo leader

events.title: Hong Kong apartment fires: hearings to begin into Wang Fuk blaze that killed 168 people
events.description: Independent committee to investigate safety standards and whether building practices contributed to worst residential fires in decades How the Hong Kong fire unfolded – visual guide Public hearings in Hong Kong begin on Thursday into a devastating fire that ripped through a housing complex last year, killing 168 people . A judge-led independent committee will investigate whether fire safety standards were inadequate, if construction practices contributed to the fire, and if there were failures on the part of government officers or contractors. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Hong Kong apartment fires: hearings to begin into Wang Fuk blaze that killed 168 people

events.title: Bernardi vows to pay for flights taken with Hanson on Rinehart’s plane amid confusion about SA’s donations ban
events.description: World-leading laws to be tested ahead of South Australian state election, complicated by Hanson and Bernardi’s political status Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Cory Bernardi says he will pay for multiple flights with Pauline Hanson in a plane registered to Gina Rinehart’s company amid confusion about whether the trips may contravene South Australia’s new laws banning political donations. Saturday’s SA election is the first since the new laws came into effect. There are a range of exemptions to the ban, but it is not clear if any of them apply to One Nation as parties, candidates and the electoral commission work through the “world-leading” laws for the first time. Continue reading...

events.title: Canada wants to build up its long-neglected Arctic. The hard question is how
events.description: Ottawa wants to modernize a region in the north that’s about six times the size of Texas, ‘just like in the 1800s’ Picture an Arctic territory, marginalized by its own country, almost entirely lacking roads, ports and power sources, but rich in mining potential and suddenly feeling vulnerable to outside threats. It’s not Greenland ; it’s the Canadian Arctic. Continue reading...

events.title: Senegal stripped of AFCON title: Reactions in Dakar
events.description: In a shock, historic decision, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) announced that it is withdrawing the Africa Cup of Nations trophy from Senegal, awarding it instead to Morocco. events.title: Deepfakes, voice cloning and weaponised AI: Global wake-up call to organised fraud

As their retirement age approached, the idea of a low-risk investment for their pension seemed attractive. But one day, after clicking on a seemingly legitimate online advert that offered a reasonable risk-averse plan, they unlocked a process that would lead them to lose over $2.5 million.

events.title: Woman has sentence quashed by Tanzania court after over a decade on death row
events.description: Lemi Limbu, who has severe intellectual disabilities, remains in prison and will now face retrial for the murder of her daughter A woman with severe intellectual disabilities in Tanzania has had her conviction and death sentence quashed after spending more than a decade in prison awaiting execution. Lemi Limbu , now in her early 30s, was convicted of the murder of her daughter in 2015. On 4 March, a court in Shinyanga, northern Tanzania, declared she can appeal. She will face a retrial, but a date has yet to be set. Continue reading...

events.title: Looking for leverage: China keeps close eye on US politics after summit delay
events.description: Beijing seeks to decipher effect of Iran war on US midterms and best way to apply pressure when Trump meets Xi The White House said on Wednesday that China had agreed to postpone Donald Trump’s visit to Beijing, as war in the Middle East rages on, complicating the US president’s position at home and abroad. China has not yet commented on the delay to the highly anticipated trip, in which Trump and the Chinese president, Xi Jinping, will meet in person for the first time since October. Trump previously said he hoped to delay the trip, originally scheduled to run from 31 March to 2 April, for “five or six weeks”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Looking for leverage: China keeps close eye on US politics after summit delay

events.description: Bola Tinubu became the first Nigerian president to deliver remarks at Windsor Castle, attending a state banquet held in his honor by King Charles. Tinubu is on a two-day state ​visit to the UK.

events.title: Cyprus leader calls for frank discussion on 'colonial' UK bases
events.description: The UK's two military bases on Cyprus are a "colonial consequence" on the island, says President Nikos Christodoulides.

events.title: India's young are more educated than ever. So why are so many jobless?
events.description: India’s educational enrolment has surged but jobs, especially good ones, are not keeping pace, a new report finds.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Archaeological site in Chile upends theory of how humans populated the Americas … again
events.description: Discovery at Monte Verde puts north-to-south expansion theory back at centre of heated debate on continent’s human history A groundbreaking new study may have once again upended our understanding of human prehistory in the Americas. For years, the predominant theory of how humans arrived in the western hemisphere centred around the Clovis culture, which crossed the Beringia land bridge from Asia between 13,400 and 12,800 years ago, and spread south. Continue reading...

events.title: Access to RSS feeds is rejected
events.description: Your large amount of concurrent requests for RSS data causes problems. Use the API or contact ops@reliefweb.int

events.title: Russia ships fuel to Cuba using 'spoofing' tactic challenging Trump embargo: reports
events.description: Hong Kong-flagged tanker allegedly delivered 190,000 barrels of Russian oil to Cuba using AIS spoofing and ship-to-ship transfers to evade U.S. sanctions.

events.title: Pro-life leader criticizes 'insane' UK bill that would decriminalize certain abortions up until birth
events.description: Shawn Carney, CEO of 40 Days for Life, expressed concern over a U.K. bill aimed at shielding women from criminal liability for abortions up until birth, describing it as "insane."

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Power prices expected to fall by up to 10% from July, bringing ‘welcome relief’ to Australia’s east coast
events.description: Increased wind and solar generation, along with falling electricity contract prices, are expected to deliver lower energy bills Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Power prices on Australia’s east coast are predicted to fall from July because of increased output from wind generation and batteries, and falling electricity contract prices, with potential savings up to $1,320 for some small businesses. In a draft decision on Thursday, the Australia Energy Regulator (AER) proposed a price reduction for customers on standing electricity plans – known as the “default market offer” – of between 1.3% to 10.1% for residential customers, and between 8.5% and 21.2% for small businesses, depending on the region. Continue reading...

events.title: Mumbai celebrates local new year
events.description: #Mumbai rang with drums as it celebrated the #GudiPadwa festival, which marks the start of the new year in #Marathi culture and the arrival of spring. "Gudi" references the flag of Lord Brahma, creator of the universe according to Hindus, and "Padwa" means the first day of the lunar phase.

events.title: French mayors: Throwing in the sash?
events.description: The second round of French municipal elections are taking place this coming weekend, but the vast majority of mayors – particularly in small towns – have already been elected. In this edition of Entre Nous, we take a look at what the job entails, why so many mayors say they are exhausted, why so many of them have quit in the past term... and how to properly wear a mayoral sash.

events.title: Party chaos caught on camera as police push new tactic to scare spring breakers
events.description: Spring break Florida crackdown sees Bay County Sheriff Tommy Ford's viral body camera video campaign deter college students from unlawful behavior in Panama City Beach.

events.title: Kevin Spacey and accusers settle before civil trial
events.description: The actor agrees an out-of-court settlement with three men who accused him of sexual assault.

events.title: Labubu film is official with Paddington director at the helm
events.description: The film will combine live action and computer-generated animation and is in "early development".

events.title: Afghan evacuees in limbo in Qatar camp accuse US of betrayal
events.description: More than 1,100 Afghans evacuated by the US fear their route to promised resettlement is closed for good.

events.title: Famous Iditarod dog sled race ends in repeat win for Jessie Holmes
events.description: The former reality TV star guided his dogs through a roughly 1,000-mile (1,609-kilometer) race, completing it in nine days, seven hours, and 32 minutes.

events.title: China’s silence on Iran reveals its true priorities
events.description: Beijing's muted response shows that when core interests are at stake, even close partners are expendable.

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Fear, defiance, and anger: Iranians describe life under bombardment
events.description: In messages to NPR, Tehran residents describe largely deserted streets roamed by paramilitary officials and vigilantes. They say security forces are banning gatherings for Nowruz, the Persian new year, this week.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fear, defiance, and anger: Iranians describe life under bombardment

events.title: Germany considers windfall tax amid rising fuel prices
events.description: Germany is set to pass legislation to put the brakes on rapidly rising gas prices. Berlin says a tax on petroleum profiteers is under consideration, but adds that it's too early for any drastic action.

events.title: Migrants drown after being dropped by smugglers off Comoros
events.description: At least 18 people drowned while trying to reach the French island of Mayotte. Many others were stranded hundreds of kilometers short of their destination.

events.title: Iranians out in strength as thousands attend funeral for security chief and navy crew
events.description: As Iran's government buried its fallen heroes, its supporters came out in strength.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iranians out in strength as thousands attend funeral for security chief and navy crew

events.title: Social media blamed for stark decline in young people's happiness
events.description: Heavy social media use has led to a stark drop in happiness among young people, according to a major academic study.

events.title: Senegal vows to appeal 'unfair, unacceptable' ruling stripping it of African football trophy
events.description: Senegal says it will appeal the "unfair, unprecedented, and unacceptable decision" to strip it of its Africa Cup of Nations title after a chaotic final against host Morocco.

events.title: China not planning to invade Taiwan in 2027, US says
events.description: China is not currently planning to invade Taiwan next year, according to US spy agencies.

events.title: Senegal stripped of Africa Cup of Nations
events.description: Senegal has been stripped of the Africa Cup of Nations for misconduct in the tournament final, the Confederation of African Football (CAF) has ruled.

events.title: Mexico arrests suspect wanted in the 2023 killing of Ecuadorian candidate and sends him to Colombia
events.description: Mexican authorities have arrested an Ecuadorian fugitive accused of helping organize the 2023 assassination of presidential candidate Fernando Villavicencio

events.title: A real possum appears among plush toy animals in Australian airport gift shop
events.description: On shelves crammed with cuddly toy native animals in an Australian airport gift shop, one fluffy possum stood out because its brown eyes were moving

events.title: Japan records trade surplus as export growth balances out weak China demand
events.description: Japan has recorded a trade surplus of 57.3 billion yen, or $360 million, in February, according to government data, reversing from a deficit a month earlier

events.title: Congo and Rwanda agree on steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo
events.description: Congolese and Rwandan officials have agreed on coordinated steps to de-escalate tensions in eastern Congo where Congo is fighting rebels backed by the neighboring Rwanda

events.title: EU leaders slam Orbán over Ukraine loan veto, accuse election games
events.description: EU leaders are accusing Hungary’s Viktor Orbán of blocking major aid to Ukraine to score points at home ahead of elections next month

events.title: Man arrested after climbing into pygmy hippo Moo Deng's enclosure at Thai zoo
events.description: A Thai zoo said police has arrested a man after he entered the enclosure of Moo Deng, the viral pygmy hippo

events.title: Guterres urges action against ‘rising tide of anti-Muslim hatred’
events.description: The UN chief on Monday urged countries to “work together” and eradicate a rising tide of anti-Muslim hate, calling for a rejection of “the narratives of fear and exclusion”.

events.title: Florida deputies mock rowdy spring breakers with NCAA-style scoreboard as arrests surge
events.description: Florida sheriff's office goes viral with hilarious posts targeting spring break students, creating NCAA tournament-style arrest leaderboard.

events.title: Suspect accused of killing NYPD's Jonathan Diller 'looks like he's smiling' in bodycam video: testimony
events.description: Trial testimony on Wednesday revealed disturbing details as Guy Rivera faces murder charges for allegedly gunning down NYPD officer Jonathan Diller in March 2024.

events.title: Florida hospital sues to evict patient who refuses to leave for months
events.description: A Florida hospital is seeking a court order to remove a patient who has allegedly refused to leave for 5 months after being discharged.

events.title: Colorado school staffer accused of sexually abusing 13-year-old student, now on the run
events.description: A Colorado school staffer allegedly abused a 13-year-old student for months. She is now on the run, facing 12 felony charges including sexual assault on a child.

events.title: Millionaire developer found dead in ritzy beach home; bizarrely dressed suspect arrested after standoff
events.description: A millionaire developer, 92, was found dead in a Southern California beach home after a bizarrely dressed suspect surrendered after an hours-long SWAT standoff, authorities say.

events.title: Video shows suspect’s desperate river escape as officers launch paddleboard pursuit
events.description: Florida officers used paddleboards to arrest a fleeing suspect who jumped into the Halifax River. Chase Cruz faces loitering and resisting arrest charges.

events.title: Ex-principal sidelined over Charlie Kirk controversy now headed to prison in illicit image case
events.description: Former Rainier High principal sentenced to over 5 years for possessing child sexual abuse material. Jeremy Williams must register as sex offender.

events.title: Venezuela's Delcy Rodriguez replaces sanctioned loyalist defense minister with military intel head
events.description: Acting president Delcy Rodriguez has appointed Gustavo Gonzalez Lopez to lead the defense ministry, a move to consolidate military control.

events.title: Multiple allies decline US calls for Strait of Hormuz support amid rising Middle East tensions
events.description: Several U.S. allies are refusing military involvement in securing the Strait of Hormuz, citing diplomacy, legal limits or strategic caution.
